source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Consider a one-dimensional periodic potential $U(x)$ viewed as an array of identical potential barriers $V(x)$ of width $a$, centered at the points $x= \\pm n a$ where $n$ is an integer. The barrier $V(x)$, schematically drawn in Fig. 1.19, may be characterized by a transmission coefficient $t(k)$ and a reflection coefficient $r(k)$ for an electron incident on the barrier with energy $E=\\frac{\\hbar^{2} \\kappa^{2}}{2 m}$. Suppose the barriers are weak, i.e., $|t|=1$ and $|r|=0$, find a simple expression for the width of the energy gaps.

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和图像资产均存在，且资产登记通过
无已登记问题或风险标记，核心语义支撑完整
答案可验证性较高，适合直接进入候选池

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Consider a one-dimensional periodic potential $U(x)$ viewed as an array of identical potential barriers $V(x)$ of width $a$, centered at the points $x= \\pm n a$ where $n$ is an integer. The barrier $V(x)$, schematically drawn in Fig. 1.19, may be characterized by a transmission coefficient $t(k)$ and a reflection coefficient $r(k)$ for an electron incident on the barrier with energy $E=\\frac{\\hbar^{2} \\kappa^{2}}{2 m}$. Suppose the barriers are weak, i.e., $|t|=1$ and $|r|=0$, find a simple expression for the width of the energy gaps.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

metadata.reasoning_chain:
Consider an energy gap in the $k$-space with boundaries $k_{1}, k_{2}$ and midpoint at $k_{0}$ and width $2 \epsilon$. It can be seen from Fig. 1.20 and Eq. (2) that
\[
 egin{aligned}
\left|\cos \left(k_{0} a+\deltaight)ight| & =1 \
\left|\cos \left(k_{0} a+\delta+\epsilon aight)ight| & =|t|
\end{aligned}
\]
or
\[
k_{0} a+\delta=n \pi
\]
where $n$ is zero or an integer, and
\[
|\sin (n \pi+\epsilon a)|=\sqrt{1-|t|^{2}}=|r|
\]

For a weak barrier, $|r| ightarrow 0,|t| ightarrow 1,|\delta| ightarrow 0$, and as can be seen from Fig. 1.20, $\epsilon a ightarrow 0$, so that
\[
k_{0}= rac{1}{2}\left(k_{2}+k_{1}ight) \approx  rac{n \pi}{2 a}
\]
and
\[
|\sin (n \pi) \cos (\epsilon a)+\sin (\epsilon a) \cos (n \pi)| \approx \epsilon a=|r|
\]
i.e.,
\[
k_{2}-k_{1}= rac{|r|}{a}
\]

Hence the energy gap has width
\[
\Delta E= rac{\hbar^{2}}{2 m}\left(k_{2}^{2}-k_{1}^{2}ight)= rac{\hbar^{2}}{m} k_{0}\left(k_{2}-k_{1}ight)= rac{n \pi}{2}  rac{\hbar^{2}}{m a^{2}}|r|
\]
